## Task
BUG found minutes after TYPED-TASKS-001 merged (2026-07-10, reproduced twice): the ID generator does not increment past an existing generated ID. Repro: `task create --type store ...` correctly created STORE-001; the NEXT `task create --type store ...` proposed STORE-001 again and failed with "packet already exists: STORE-001" — deterministically. Expected: STORE-002.
Likely cause (verify, do not assume): the max-existing-number query for the prefix fails to match/parse the just-created `STORE-001` (regex or type-column filter misses it), so next = 001 forever once any generated ID exists for a type.
Fix:
1. Diagnose the actual cause with a failing test FIRST (the RED below reproduces the exact incident).
2. The generator must compute max over ALL packets whose id matches ^<PREFIX>-(\d+)$ regardless of how they were created (import/rebuild/create), plus a DB uniqueness constraint so a collision can NEVER insert (defense in depth — TYPED-TASKS-001 scoped the constraint; verify it exists, add it if the worker skipped it and record that as a review miss).
3. Add the red-team case: rapid sequential creates of the same type never collide.

## RED test (write first)
In src/tasks/service.test.ts add a test named exactly: "sequential creates of the same type increment the generated id past existing ones". Create two packets with the same --type and assert ids NNN and NNN+1; also pre-seed an imported packet with a high number (e.g. STORE-041) and assert the next create yields STORE-042. Today the second create collides -> it FAILS.
Expected failure cause (literal string in the output): the test name "sequential creates of the same type increment the generated id past existing ones".

## Reuse
The generator + type->prefix map from TYPED-TASKS-001; the store uniqueness machinery.

## Stop conditions
Patching the symptom (e.g. retry-on-collision) instead of fixing the max computation; skipping the DB uniqueness constraint; touching files outside the write_set.

## Evidence required at close
red-test-output, verify-root, final-sha.
